title: Inspector panels: put name input on same row as close button to reclaim vertical space
description:
## Problem

Every inspector panel renders a dedicated row at the top just for the close (X) button, then a small vertical gap, then the name input as the first row of the inspector body. That dedicated row plus the gap wastes a noticeable strip of vertical space directly above the entity name on every panel (Context, Group, User, UserNeed, Team, FlowStage; Relationship and the connection inspectors also pay the same chrome cost).

This is most visible on a freshly created entity where only the name is set: the panel shows a band of empty space, then the name, then the rest of the panel.

## Source of the wasted space

`src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx:15-27` defines `CloseButton` as a `div` with `flex justify-end -mt-1 -mr-1 mb-2`. It is always rendered above whichever specific inspector is chosen (line 98), so every inspector inherits this fixed first row.

Each editable-name inspector then starts its own JSX with an `<input className={INPUT_TITLE_CLASS}>` as the first element. So we always pay: close-button row + `mb-2` gap + name row.

## Goal

Render the close (X) button on the same row as the entity name input, right-aligned. The name input grows to fill the row; the X sits to its right. No gap above the name.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Every inspector panel that currently shows an editable name (Context, Group, User, UserNeed, Team, FlowStage) renders the name input and the close button on a single horizontal row at the top of the panel, with no row above the name.
2. The close-button hit target stays roughly the same size as today (currently `p-1.5` around a 16px X icon); accessibility is preserved (`aria-label="Close inspector"`).
3. For inspectors without an editable name (Relationship, UserNeedConnection, NeedContextConnection), the close button still appears in the top-right corner, but no longer takes a whole row when something else can share it; if there is nothing else, an inspector-supplied header (e.g. a small label) sits beside it.
4. Visually, this should look like a tightened version of the existing layout, not a redesign. Same fonts, same spacing rhythm below the new top row.
5. The Delete button at the bottom of each inspector is untouched.

## Implementation options (open question; pick during implementation)

The chrome-vs-content split makes the right factoring nontrivial because the name input lives inside each inspector body today:

- **Slot approach**: `InspectorPanel` accepts a `topRow` (or `title`) slot from the chosen inspector and renders it on the same flex row as the X. Each inspector exports its name input via that slot. Cleanest separation, touches every inspector once.
- **Move close into each inspector**: each inspector renders its own top row with name + X. Duplication across inspectors, but no shared-API change. Probably the wrong tradeoff long-term.
- **Hoist the name into the panel**: have `InspectorPanel` own the name input itself, with a per-entity wiring layer. Bigger refactor; out of scope unless we also want to consolidate other shared chrome.

Recommend the slot approach. Smallest change to `InspectorPanel.tsx`, predictable update at the top of each inspector body, no behavioral changes.

## Files in scope

- `src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx` (replace `CloseButton` with a header row that accepts a left-side child)
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx`, `GroupInspector.tsx`, `UserInspector.tsx`, `UserNeedInspector.tsx`, `TeamInspector.tsx`, `FlowStageInspector.tsx` (lift the name input out of the body into the new header slot, or render at top with shared styling so it lines up)
- `src/components/inspector/RelationshipInspector.tsx`, `UserNeedConnectionInspector.tsx`, `NeedContextConnectionInspector.tsx` (no name to lift; confirm header still renders sanely with just the X, or expose a small label)
- Inspector tests in `src/components/inspector/__tests__/` (mostly query `getByDisplayValue` for the name; layout change should not break those; confirm)

## Out of scope

- Reordering content within an inspector body.
- Restyling the close-button glyph or hover behavior.
- Any logic around when the panel opens or closes.

title: Review ContextFlow PR #20 (ES view from Angel Vazquez)
description: Review and respond to community PR adding EventStorming view to ContextFlow. Analysis saved in products/research/contextflow/2026-03-29-es-view-pr-analysis.md. Need to: (1) do a code review pass, (2) draft a thoughtful reply explaining why it doesn't align with product direction, (3) point Angel toward EventStormer as alternative.

title: Allow much tighter zoom-in on canvas
description: React Flow zoom is currently clamped at maxZoom=2 in src/components/CanvasArea.tsx:1012, which blocks users from zooming in tighter than 200%. Raise the ceiling significantly (e.g., 4-8x) so users can zoom in close to inspect a single context, read small text, or work in dense areas of the map. Also consider whether minZoom (currently 0.1) needs adjustment for very large maps. Verify overlay cutoffs (issue labels, team labels at zoom < 0.4) and node rendering still look reasonable at the new bounds.

title: Bug: dragging contexts in Strategic/Distillation views does not auto-classify
description: Moving a context in Strategic view should auto-update evolutionStage (genesis/custom-built/product/commodity) based on x position. Moving in Distillation view should auto-update strategicClassification (core/supporting/generic). The logic exists in contextActions.ts moveContextAction (lines 113-126) using classifyFromStrategicPosition and classifyFromDistillationPosition, but this is dead code. The store's updateContextPosition just calls getCollabMutations().updateContextPosition() with no classification. The distillation view has partial logic in setViewMode (runs on view switch only, not during drag). Fix: add auto-classification to the collab mutation code path, likely in updateContextPosition or as a post-drag handler similar to completeFlowStageMove.

title: Excessive tooltip layering on relationship edge hover
description: Hovering a context relationship edge shows three overlapping affordances for the same fact: (1) the inline pattern label on the edge ("Partnership"), (2) a native browser <title> tooltip ("partnership") from <title>{pattern}</title> at src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx:328, (3) a custom portal-rendered educational tooltip (dark card with title, description, characteristics) at src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx:330-371. The native <title> also violates the project rule in CLAUDE.md ("NEVER use native title attribute"). Proposed fix: remove the <title> element entirely; keep the educational portal tooltip but only on first-time hover or when showHelpTooltips is on, and consider a short delay so it does not stack visually with the inline label. Alternatively, suppress the educational tooltip on hover when the edge already has a visible inline label, and reserve it for the indicator-box patterns (ACL/OHS) where the meaning is less obvious. Goal: one clear affordance per hover, not three.

title: Connection UX redesign: hover stubs, whole-shape drop targets (GH #22)
description:
Redesign relationship creation UX for the DDD Europe 2026 workshop audience. The current implementation renders two fixed source-handle dots on each context's left and right edges; users must precisely target them to start a connection, and the resulting edge's display geometry disagrees with where they dragged from. This bead replaces that with discoverable hover affordances and a forgiving drop target.

Three implementation changes:

1) Hover stubs (draw.io style). On hover, show directional arrow nubs on all 4 sides of a context (top, right, bottom, left). Nubs are translucent (~35% opacity) by default and pop to full opacity plus accent color on individual hover. A small tooltip appears on stub hover: "Drag to another context to map the relationship". Drag from any stub starts a connection. Replaces the existing fixed left/right source-handle dots.

2) Whole-shape drop target. While a connection-drag is in flight, dropping anywhere on the body of a destination context completes the connection. Implemented as a single transparent full-size React Flow target Handle behind the visible card so React Flow's existing hit-testing accepts the drop. The displayed endpoint floats to the closest side via the joint-anchor algorithm from #21. Body-drag of a card continues to move it: connection-drag and node-drag stay distinct because connection-drag is initiated by a stub mousedown, node-drag by a body mousedown.

3) Bezier curves preserved. The hand-drawn/organic feel is intentional for strategic-design work; orthogonal routing was considered and rejected. No change to path type.

Coupled deliverables that ship in the same PR:

- Arrow marker size: bump relationship arrow markers from markerWidth/markerHeight 5 to 8 (markers defined in src/components/CanvasArea.tsx around lines 1063-1119, all five marker variants). Reads better at workshop zoom levels.
- Rich card-hover tooltip guidance line: update the line in src/lib/contextTooltip.ts that currently reads "Drag handles to connect to other contexts" to "Drag any arrow to another context to map the relationship." This help text describes the changing affordance and will go stale if we ship the new affordance without updating it.

Key files to touch (orientation, not prescription): src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx (replace fixed source handles with hover stubs, add per-stub tooltip), src/components/CanvasArea.tsx (marker defs, onConnect flow), src/lib/contextTooltip.ts (guidance line wording), src/lib/edgeGeometry.ts (already correct via #21; no change expected). Existing User -> User Need -> Context flow uses the top target handle and must keep working.

Background and decisions: docs/connection-ux-research.md and docs/mockups/connection-ux-mockup.html. These currently live on the mockup/connection-ux branch (htmlpreview link on GH #22 comment); both will land in the implementation PR or its predecessor merge.

Direction convention is kept as-is for #22 (drag-from = downstream, drag-to = upstream; arrow points to upstream, as documented in src/model/types.ts:80-86). Reopen during two-sided pattern work (contextflow-ki1 / GH #13). All copy on the new creation surfaces is intentionally direction-neutral so symmetric patterns (Partnership, Shared Kernel, Separate Ways, which render with no arrow head) read correctly.

Acceptance criteria (observable behavior):
- Hovering a context reveals 4 translucent arrow nubs on top/right/bottom/left.
- Hovering one nub pops it to full opacity and shows tooltip "Drag to another context to map the relationship".
- Dragging from any nub starts a connection-drag with a ghost line following the cursor.
- Dropping on any point of a destination context's body (not just a handle) creates the relationship.
- Body-drag of a context (not on a nub) moves the context; no relationship is created.
- The displayed edge endpoint floats to the closest side per #21 (no regression).
- Relationship arrow markers render visibly larger than before (markerWidth/markerHeight 8 vs prior 5).
- The rich card-hover tooltip (gated by showHelpTooltips) shows updated guidance: "Drag any arrow to another context to map the relationship." No reference to "handles" remains.
- Existing User -> User Need -> Context connection flow still works.
- Symmetric patterns (Partnership, Shared Kernel, Separate Ways) still render with no arrow head.
- The original two fixed left/right source-handle dots are no longer rendered.

Separate decision flagged for the user (not in current scope):
- First-run coach mark teaching the gesture. This is an onboarding overlay, not a tooltip. Discussed during mockup review. Ask before pulling in.

See GH issue #22 for product framing and workshop motivation. Depends on contextflow-j7k (closed: joint anchor side selection for floating edges).

title: Fix bezier line and arrow marker overlap at edge endpoint (GH #24)
description: The bezier path runs to the destination anchor on the box edge, and the arrow marker is drawn on top, causing line+marker visual overlap. Path should stop at the back of the arrow triangle so only the point touches the box. Two implementation options: (1) geometrically shorten the path by arrow length in RelationshipEdge.tsx or getEdgeParams, or (2) configure SVG marker refX to position the marker base at the path endpoint. Independent of #21 but sequencing-wise queue after #21. See GH #24.

title: Set up Cloudflare billing usage notifications
description: After exceeding the free tier Durable Objects limits (2026-02-26), upgraded to paid plan. Need to configure billing usage alerts in Cloudflare dashboard to avoid unexpected charges. Set up notifications for Workers, Durable Objects, and Pages usage thresholds.

title: ARCHITECTURE.md: add Distillation View throughout
description:

title: Audit and remove dead action functions superseded by collab mutations
description: The store was refactored to use collab mutations (getCollabMutations()) but the old action functions in src/model/actions/ still exist with stale trackEvent calls. context_added, user_added, user_need_added events were silently dropped because the store methods were the real code path. Audit all action files (contextActions.ts, userActions.ts, etc.) to identify which functions are dead code vs still called, and remove the dead ones. Single source of truth should be the store methods.
close_reason: Deleted 4 dead action files (contextActions, userActions, teamActions, temporalActions) plus their tests, removed unused userActions import from store.ts. 3,352 lines removed. projectActions, projectHelpers, and keyframeHelpers kept (still actively used).

title: Context inspector: panel scroll locks when an expanded repo card is open
description:
## Symptom

In the Context inspector, expanding a repo card (clicking the GitBranch chip in the Repos section) causes the inspector panel itself to stop scrolling. With the repo card collapsed, the inspector scrolls normally; with it expanded, the user cannot reach content above or below the visible viewport, even though there is clearly more panel content offscreen.

This blocks editing for any context that has both:
- A repo with non-trivial expanded content (contributor lists, stats, etc.), AND
- Content above or below that the user needs to reach (Notes textarea, Relationships, Delete button, name/purpose, etc.).

## Where to look

- The inspector panel host: `src/App.tsx:340` renders the right sidebar as `<aside className="... overflow-y-auto ...">` wrapping `<InspectorPanel />`. That overflow on the aside is the scroll surface.
- The repo card itself: `src/components/inspector/ContextRepoCard.tsx`. Toggled via the `expandedRepoId` state owned by `ContextInspector.tsx`. The expanded view renders contributor lists and stats; need to confirm none of those use a layout that clips or steals scroll events from the parent aside.
- `ContextInspector.tsx` lines 371-398 host the repo cards inside a `space-y-2` wrapper inside the Codebase `SectionDivider`. Need to check whether any ancestor has `overflow-hidden`, a fixed `height`, or a flex container with a missing `min-h-0` that would break the parent scroll.

## Likely suspects (verify before fixing)

- An expanded card with its own `overflow-hidden` or scrollable inner container, which can swallow wheel events in some browsers when the cursor is over the card.
- A nested flex/grid where the panel grows to fit content instead of overflowing, because a parent uses `h-full` without `min-h-0`.
- An `overflow-y-auto` accidentally set on the expanded repo content instead of letting the aside handle it.
- A mouse-wheel handler somewhere in the contributor list or stats sub-component that calls `stopPropagation`.

## Repro

1. Run `npm run dev:local`.
2. Open a project that has a context with at least one assigned repo, plus enough Notes + Relationships + Delete content to require panel scroll. (Any of the built-in samples should work.)
3. Select the context. Confirm the inspector panel scrolls normally.
4. Click the repo chip to expand it. Try to scroll the inspector. The panel no longer scrolls; the content above (name) and below (delete) is unreachable.
5. Collapse the repo. Panel scroll returns.

## Acceptance criteria

1. With a repo card expanded, the inspector panel still scrolls normally; the user can reach the name input at the top and the Delete button at the bottom by scrolling.
2. The expanded repo card may have its own internal scrolling for long contributor lists if desired, but it must not break the outer panel scroll.
3. Behavior is the same in light and dark mode, and in all current view modes (Flow / Strategic / Distillation).
4. No regression to the collapsed repo card layout.

## Files in scope (starting points)

- `src/components/inspector/ContextRepoCard.tsx`
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx`
- `src/App.tsx` (the aside that owns the scroll surface)

## Notes

This may share a root cause with contextflow-pmt (the inspector chrome / scroll surface is a small but real surface for layout bugs). Worth landing both together if the fix touches the same lines.
